rulings.Case Title: CARYL HARRISON VS. DEREK A. TAGGARD MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers And Production Of Documents At Deposition; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defts And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 25, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF CARYL HARRISON'S Motion To Compel Answers And Production Of Documents At Deposition; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And His Attorney. Denied. Defendant Derek A. Taggard is not disclosed an as expert in the instant action, and the questions asked by Plaintiff's counsel seeks to extract an impermissible after-the-fact impression of the CT scan taken on October 17, 2011. "Questions to the defendant physicians about their impressions and reasons for their action or lack of action at the time the medical procedure was performed are, of course, entirely appropriate. Such questions were asked without objection by defense counsel. But, as the Scarano case makes clear, questions about after-the-fact opinions and impressions of the physicians stand in quite another light." (County of Los Angeles v. Sup.Ct. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1446, 1455-56.) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GUSTAVO RIOS VS. CERCLE DE L'UNION, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Based On Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Courts April 7th Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, June 25, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT CERCLE DE L'UNION, DBA THE FRENCH CLUB's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Based On Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Courts April 7th Order Compelling Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THIS COURT'S ORDER OF APRIL 7, 2014 COMPELLING PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE TO DISCOERY MATTERS. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT WILL SIGN AN ORDER DISMISSING PLAINTIFF'S ACTION TO THE PROVISIONS OF SECTION 22023.030(d)(3) UNLESS WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF FILES FOR AND COMPLETE RESPONSES CALLED FOR BY THE COURT'S ORDER OF APRIL 7TH AND PAYS THE MONETARY SANCTIONS IMPOSED IN THAT ORDER. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, DELIVER A COPY TO PLAINTIFF AND PRESENT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON JUNE 25, 2014 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD DEVINE VS. ROSALBA NAVARRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 25, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT ROSALBA NAVARRO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. As to the statute of limitations argument, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") alleges that payment on the loan was due when Defendant "was [financially] able to do so." (FAC, par. 12.) The FAC alleges that Defendant was financially able to do so in June 2013. (FAC, par. 14.) The two-year statute of limitations began running in June 2013, or when this condition precedent was met. The complaint was filed in January 2014, and is therefore timely. As to the statute of frauds argument, the one year provision "applies to those contracts which, by their terms, cannot possibly be performed within one year." (White Lighting Co. v. Wolf (1968) 68 Cal.2d 336, 343.) As pled, this contract could have been performed within one year. Further, this was a contract between two individuals without any third party guaranty. As such, Civ. Code sec. 1624(a)(2) does not bar the claim. Lastly, the FAC is not so uncertain that Defendant cannot reasonably respond. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:85.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT ARTS COUNCIL VS. CITY AND CONTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Prelim Inujunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 25, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF BAYVIEW HUNTERS POINT ARTS COUNCIL'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Denied. Plaintiff failed to join indispensable parties, the grantees. (CCP § 389(a).) To the extent that Plaintiff attempted to cure this defect by filing a First Amended Complaint and serving a summons, the motion is untimely. Plaintiff failed to timely serve the motion on the grantees. (CCP § 1005(b).) rulings.Case Title: JADE GROUP, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. KWAN & ASSOCIATES, A BUSINESS ORGANIZATION OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 25, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT G B & E CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend in part and overruled in part. As to the first cause of action for negligence, the demurrer is overruled as the complaint adequately pleads the elements of this cause of action and is not fatally uncertain. As to the second cause of action for equitable indemnity, the demurrer is overruled because the complaint adequately pleads that Defendant's negligent conduct contributed to Plaintiff having to settle with the individual homeowners. As to the third cause of action for breach of contract, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The complaint does not adequately plead the material terms of the contract. Additionally, the complaint does not specify whether the contract entered into with Defendant was written or oral. (CCP sec. 430.10(g).) As to the fourth cause of action for breach of implied warranty, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Because the demurrer does not adequately plead breach of contract, it follows that the complaint does not state a cause of action for breach of implied warranty. The Court did not consider Defendant's evidence and arguments raised for the first time in reply. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
